summary: Added initial C coverage for four functions in src/melee/gm/gm_1601.c, improving the large gm_1601 translation unit by a reported 0.20%. The PR implemented unlockable-character checks, time conversion into the gm datetime struct, and a stage/setup helper callback, while adding or correcting prototypes and lightweight struct definitions needed for those matches. There were no review comments in the provided slice.
what_changed: Created real contents for src/melee/gm/gm_1601.c, adding includes and implementations for gm_80164840, gm_80164ABC, gm_801692E8, and fn_80187714.
Added global zero-initialized struct gmMainLib_8015ED8C_arg0_t lbl_803B75F8 in gm_1601.c and used it to query unlockable-character state through gmMainLib_8015ED8C.
Defined NUM_UNLOCKABLE_CHARACTERS as 11 and used it consistently for unlockable-character loops.
Implemented gm_80164840(u8 id) as an unlock check that maps an id through lbl_803B75F8.id and lbl_803B75F8.thing entries, then tests the unlockable-character bitfield returned by gmMainLib_8015ED8C.
Implemented gm_80164ABC(struct gmMainLib_8015ED8C_arg0_t* arg0) as an all-unlockables-unlocked check by verifying bits 0 through 10 in the u16 bitfield returned by gmMainLib_8015ED8C.

Implemented gm_801692E8(uint ticks, struct datetime* datetime) by converting ticks through lbTime_8000B028 into OSCalendarTime fields, adjusting month to 1-based, and clamping seconds above 59 down to 59.

Implemented fn_80187714 as a setup/reset routine calling Camera_80028B9C(6), lb_8000FCDC, mpColl_80041C78, Ground_801C0378(64), Stage_802251E8(0, NULL), Stage_8022524C, Item_80266FA8, Item_80266FCC, efLib_8005B4B8, and efAsync_8006737C(0).
Changed gm_80164ABC declaration in gm_1601.h from taking no arguments to taking struct gmMainLib_8015ED8C_arg0_t*.
Changed gm_801692E8 declaration in gm_1601.h from int to uint ticks.
Added forward declaration typedef for struct gmMainLib_8015ED8C_arg0_t in gm/forward.h and defined the partial struct layout in gm/types.h.
Added prototype for gmMainLib_8015ED8C in gmmain_lib.h and changed that header's include guard from _gmmain_lib_h_ to GALE01_15CC34.
Updated Ground_801C0378 signature from void(void) to void(int) in ground.c and ground.h to support the call Ground_801C0378(64).
Updated an existing ground.c call site to gm_80164ABC(/* TODO*/ 0) after the prototype change, preserving buildability while marking the argument as unresolved.
Added missing prototypes for Camera_80028B9C, efLib_8005B4B8, and lb_8000FCDC.

Updated config/GALE01/scratches.txt: added a new lbl_8013D764 scratch entry by ribbanya and converted several gm_1601-related scratch statuses from percentage values to OK, including gm_80164840, gm_80164ABC, func_801692E8, and func_80187714.
smart_moves: Introduced only the partial gmMainLib_8015ED8C_arg0_t layout needed by the matched code, using padding and small named fields instead of overclaiming a complete structure.
Corrected function prototypes in headers as decomp evidence required, especially adding the gm_80164ABC argument and changing gm_801692E8's tick parameter to uint.
Added cross-module prototypes rather than relying on placeholders or implicit declarations, making the gm_1601 implementation reusable and type-checkable.

Kept the unresolved ground.c argument explicit with a TODO comment at gm_80164ABC(/* TODO*/ 0), which preserves a known uncertainty instead of hiding it behind a guessed global or fabricated parameter.
Captured a game-specific behavior note in gm_80164ABC: Gekko codes that unlock all characters patch the false return into true, which documents why the branch shape matters.
decomp_lessons: For large game-mode files, small high-confidence functions can still unlock progress when paired with correct prototypes and minimal supporting structs.
When a function consumes a large unknown data object, a partial struct with padding can be preferable to UNK_T if known offsets and array shapes are enough to match the function.
Bitfield unlock checks should preserve apparent original width and constants; this PR uses a u16* return from gmMainLib_8015ED8C and checks 11 unlockable-character bits.
Date/time conversion functions may require small semantic adjustments visible in assembly, such as OSCalendarTime.mon + 1 and clamping seconds to 59.
Changing a function signature in one game-mode header can force call-site edits in other systems, so matching work should include downstream prototype repair.
Scratch entries should be updated from percentage statuses to OK when matched or accepted by the local matching workflow.
naming_conventions: Used address-based names for unknown or not-yet-renamed functions: gm_80164840, gm_80164ABC, gm_801692E8, fn_80187714, Camera_80028B9C, efLib_8005B4B8, lb_8000FCDC.
Used subsystem prefixes matching existing project style: gm_, gmMainLib_, Ground_, Stage_, Item_, Camera_, efLib_, efAsync_, lb_, mpColl_.
Named the new partial argument struct gmMainLib_8015ED8C_arg0_t, tying it directly to the function that consumes it.
Used descriptive local names where evidence was strong enough, including unlockable_character_bitfield, ticks, datetime, and NUM_UNLOCKABLE_CHARACTERS.
Left uncertain struct members as pad, id, thing, unk0, unk1, and unkpad rather than assigning speculative semantic names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used a file-scope zero-initialized object, struct gmMainLib_8015ED8C_arg0_t lbl_803B75F8 = { 0 }, to match references to a known data symbol.
Matched unlock logic with a for loop over 11 entries and a break after finding the id-to-bit mapping.
Used bit tests of the form *bitfield & (1LL << index) even though the bitfield pointer is u16*, suggesting this expression shape helped match codegen.
Returned explicit true/false in gm_80164840 instead of directly returning the boolean expression, likely preserving branch/codegen structure.
For gm_801692E8, copied OSCalendarTime fields individually into struct datetime and used an if/else second clamp instead of a helper or ternary.
For fn_80187714, emitted the setup calls in a fixed order with immediate constants 6, 64, 0, NULL, and 0, matching the observed routine sequence.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The diff itself contains one unresolved note: ground.c calls gm_80164ABC(/* TODO*/ 0), indicating the correct argument at that call site was not established in this PR.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that resolve the TODO argument in ground.c for gm_80164ABC(/* TODO*/ 0).
Search for later renames or full struct recovery of gmMainLib_8015ED8C_arg0_t and lbl_803B75F8.
Check whether gm_80164840 and gm_80164ABC were later given semantic names related to unlockable characters.
Look for subsequent gm_1601.c PRs that build on fn_80187714's setup sequence or the datetime conversion helper.
Audit whether the gmmain_lib.h include guard GALE01_15CC34 remained or was later normalized.
